name: snapfire_post_wheel_shallow

routes.note: The primary, attacks the deep middle between the safeties
Sells short then turns up the sideline, cannot be pressed by a man defender
The drag underneath, the checkdown and the man-coverage outlet
Clear the deep defenders to open the post and wheel

routes.route: Read post to wheel to shallow
routes.note: Empty set, no back to check, get it out on rhythm

reads: **Pre-Snap:** Empty, read the middle-of-field safety. One high opens the post, two high opens the wheel up the sideline
**1️⃣ Post:** Take it when the deep middle is open and the verticals have cleared
**2️⃣ Wheel:** Hits up the sideline versus man (cannot be pressed) or when the corner sinks
**3️⃣ Shallow:** The underneath outlet, take the easy yards if the deep stuff is covered
usage_notes:
**Beats:** Single-high (the post) and man coverage (the wheel and shallow)
**Coach's Key:** Empty has no protection help, the ball must come out on time

name: snapfire_fake_jet_pass_power

routes.note: Despite the name this is a RUN, the power hits downhill behind the pull
Fakes the jet to hold the backside edge, no handoff
Kicks the force, the back runs off his block
Playside down-blocks and a double team up to the linebacker

reads: **Pre-Snap:** The name says pass but the art is a power RUN, the jet is a fake to freeze the edge
**1️⃣ Jet fake holds the backside:** run power downhill behind the kick
**Read the kickout:** if the end spills, bounce; if he is kicked, follow the pull inside
usage_notes:
**Beats:** Edge defenders who honor the jet, the fake removes them from the power
**Coach's Key:** This is a run, do not look to throw it

name: snapfire_power_read

routes.note: Read the end man on the line; keep through the B gap behind the wrap when he widens
The give; hits the edge with blockers in front when the read key crashes
Pull through the hole to the play-side linebacker
Backside gap rules with a double team up to the backside linebacker
Stalk the perimeter for the sweep

reads: **Pre-Snap:** A loaded box is fine here: the end man on the line goes unblocked and becomes the read key, so the extra defender is in conflict
**1️⃣ Read key crashes inside:** give the sweep, the back wins the edge with blockers in front
**2️⃣ Read key stays outside or upfield:** keep it, QB power downhill through the B gap behind the wrapping guard
**🚨 No pass option:** this is a run-run option (also called inverted veer), the only decision is give or keep
usage_notes:
**Beats:** Loaded boxes, conservative option defense (the read key plays the QB, crashes inside, and the sweep runs free)
**Coach's Key:** Hold the keep button (X on PlayStation, A on Xbox) when the read key stands up; do nothing and the give happens on its own

name: snapfire_mtn_pa_cross

routes.note: Cyan motion releasing across the formation out to the left flat
Yellow stem straight up the left side
Red primary read, breaks from the right and crosses to the left side at depth
Yellow shallow crosser working underneath to the right
Yellow release out to the right flat as the checkdown

reads: Read the deep crosser (red) first as he clears the linebackers
If the safety drives the cross, climb to the left seam
If both are covered, dump the shallow crosser or back in the flat
usage_notes:
**Beats:** Man coverage that chases the jet motion, the crosser runs free behind it
**Coach's Key:** Sell the play-action first, the red crosser is the throw

name: snapfire_midline_read_option

routes.note: Two white blocking stems releasing vertically to wall off defenders
Red primary, short keep straight up through the middle
Yellow track running laterally then upfield to the right as the give option

reads: Ride the mesh and read the interior defender
If he crashes the give, pull and keep on the red midline path
If he sits, hand it off on the yellow give track
usage_notes:
**Beats:** Aggressive interior linemen who chase the dive
**Coach's Key:** This is a run, read the key and commit

name: snapfire_mtn_mesh_traffic

routes.note: Yellow shallow crosser working left to right under the box
Yellow shallow crosser working right to left, meshing under
Cyan motion releasing into a short drag through the traffic
Yellow stem climbing up the left side behind the mesh
Yellow release out to the right flat as the checkdown

reads: Let the two crossers mesh and find the open rub
If man coverage, throw the receiver running away from his defender
If zone, hit the drag settling in the hole or take the vertical up top
usage_notes:
**Beats:** Man coverage with the natural rub at the mesh point
**Coach's Key:** Read the mesh first, the verticals are the alert against zone

name: snapfire_mesh_mills

routes.note: Red primary read, releases off the line and runs straight up the left sideline
Yellow, pushes vertical then breaks square across the middle into the open window
Yellow, shallow-to-intermediate crosser running left to right
Yellow, underneath rub working across the formation
Cyan, short settle in the middle
Cyan, releases out of the backfield as the dump option

reads: Pre-snap, eye the red streak up the left sideline: if the corner sits or you have single coverage, take the shot.
If the streak is covered over the top, work the mesh crossers and hit whichever crosser clears traffic.
If the middle is walled off, settle on the short hook or drop it to the back underneath.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage. Coach's Key: the streak clears the top while the crossing routes rub defenders, so let the mesh come open underneath.

name: snapfire_dbl_slot_cross

routes.note: Yellow vertical up the left sideline.
Yellow stem upfield then breaks hard across the field to the left.
Red primary read, shallow crossing route working left underneath.
Yellow short out breaking to the right sideline just past the line.
Yellow vertical up the right sideline.

reads: First look the red shallow crosser working across the middle.
If the underneath is covered, climb to the deep crosser coming from the left slot.
Use the two outside streaks to clear safeties and hold the corners.
usage_notes: Beats: man and zone underneath. Coach's Key: high-low the middle with the shallow drag under the deep cross.

name: snapfire_slot_drive

routes.note: Yellow stem up then hooks back toward the left sideline.
Yellow vertical straight upfield.
Yellow shallow drag working left across the formation.
Red primary read, drives upfield then breaks in hard to the left across the middle.
Yellow vertical up the right sideline.

reads: First read the red dig breaking across the middle.
If the dig is taken away, drop to the shallow drag underneath working left.
The two streaks and the comeback hold the deep defenders to open the intermediate window.
usage_notes: Beats: zone over the middle. Coach's Key: drive route on top of the drag gives a built-in high-low.

name: snapfire_four_verticals

routes.note: Yellow vertical up the left sideline.
Yellow vertical upfield (seam).
Red short curl-back over the ball, primary outlet sitting just behind the line.
Yellow vertical upfield (seam).
Yellow vertical up the right sideline turning red near the top, the marked deep shot.

reads: Read the safeties and hit the open vertical, the four go routes stretch the deep zones.
Against single high, work the seam away from the free safety.
If pressure shows, settle for the red checkdown sitting underneath.
usage_notes: Beats: single-high coverage. Coach's Key: bend the seams off the high safety and keep the back-side checkdown live.

name: snapfire_flood_drive

routes.note: Outside receiver runs straight upfield and clears out the boundary
Pushes up the field then breaks flat toward the sideline at the second level
Comes underneath across the middle on a shallow drive route
Red primary read; works upfield then snaps back toward the sideline
Releases to the right and works a shallow angle out toward the flat

reads: **Pre-Snap:** This is a flood concept stacking the right side at three levels (go, comeback, flat)
**1️⃣ Primary:** Hit the comeback on the right; it is the marked red read and your high-percentage throw vs soft corner
**2️⃣ Flood:** If the comeback is covered, climb to the deep go or drop to the flat to stress the flat defender high-low
**3️⃣ Backside:** The drive route underneath is your check-down across the middle if the right side is taken away
usage_notes: **Beats:** Zone coverage that cannot cover three levels to one side. **Coach's Key:** Read the right-side flood high to low, comeback is the primary.

name: snapfire_01_trap

routes.note: Same-side downhill through the A gap; stay patient and cut off the trap block
Backside guard pulls to kick out the first defensive lineman outside the play-side guard
Leaves the trap target alone and climbs straight to the Mike
Backside gap rules; vs odd fronts expect a double team on the nose up to a backer
Stalk the perimeter; nobody runs a route on this play

reads: **Pre-Snap:** Find the first defensive lineman outside the play-side guard; he is the trap target and the lane opens inside his vacated spot
**1️⃣ Mesh:** Straight handoff, same-side run; defenses expect shotgun runs to cross the formation, so users often fly the wrong way
**2️⃣ RB track:** Downhill through the A gap behind the down blocks; vs odd fronts the trap block on a wide 4i makes the hole huge
**🚨 Check out of it:** vs double mug or six-plus defenders stacked on the line, audible out; trap struggles vs a loaded line of scrimmage
usage_notes:
**Beats:** Odd fronts (3-3-5, Mint, Penny, 3-2-6, dime 2-3 odd), pinched lines, users jumping the inside zone stereotype
**Coach's Key:** Trap is an alternate way to run power; same gap rules, but the play-side guard free releases and the puller traps the first lineman outside him

name: snapfire_doubles_shot

routes.note: Yellow; stems up then settles back toward the sideline at a short depth
Red primary read; pushes vertical then breaks deep to the post
Yellow; bends upfield then breaks out toward the deep corner
Blue; releases to the right flat underneath
Blue; works inside across the field at a short depth
Yellow; runs straight vertical up the right sideline

reads: **Pre-Snap:** This is a shot play; the post on the left slot is the deep primary
**1️⃣ Primary:** Take the marked post on a one-high look or when the safety widens
**2️⃣ High-low:** Pair the corner over the flat to stress the boundary defender if the post is bracketed
**3️⃣ Check-down:** Settle for the drag or flat underneath if the deep shots are covered
usage_notes: **Beats:** Single-high coverage. **Coach's Key:** It is a shot play off the post, take the deep ball when the middle opens.

name: snapfire_switch_wr_drag

routes.note: Yellow; releases and works a deep crossing route up and across toward the far side
Yellow; shallow drag pushing across the middle
Yellow; releases out of the backfield to the right flat
Red primary read; runs straight up the right seam
Yellow; works out and up the right sideline on a wheel

reads: **Pre-Snap:** This is a switch-release concept with a shallow drag crossing underneath
**1️⃣ Primary:** Hit the marked vertical on the right if the seam is open vs man or split-safety
**2️⃣ Drag:** Work the shallow drag across the middle as your man-beater on the move
**3️⃣ Backside:** The deep crosser and the wheel give you a high option if underneath is jumped
usage_notes: **Beats:** Man coverage with the drag and switch release. **Coach's Key:** Vertical is the read, drag is the safety valve on the move.

name: snapfire_rpo_alert_bubble-2

routes.note: Red curling path back toward the left, the alert ball-carrier
White, lead block out front for the bubble
Yellow, quick inside slant as the alert throw off the read

reads: Pre-snap, count the numbers and leverage to the bubble side.
If the flat is light, throw the bubble screen now.
If the box defender crashes the bubble, hit the slant behind him.
Take what the defense gives, do not force it.
usage_notes: Beats: light boxes and soft edges. Coach's Key: this is a pre-snap leverage read, bubble vs slant. ART VERIFIED: red bubble curl-back left, white stalk blocks, single yellow slant from inside receiver all present; RPO label confirmed.

name: snapfire_rpo_duo_alert_post

routes.note: yellow route, breaks deep across to the post, this is the alert pass option
red ball-carrier path, runs the duo behind the down blocks, this is the primary
white blocks, down block the duo front

reads: Pre-snap: if the alert defender sits tight to the backside post WR, keep it a run.
If the box is light or the post WR has single coverage, pull and throw the post.
If the box is loaded, hand the duo and let the HB read the double team.
usage_notes: Beats: light boxes and soft backside coverage. Coach's Key: the duo run is your answer, the backside post is the alert bonus.

name: snapfire_y_trail

routes.note: yellow vertical up the left sideline
yellow shallow crosser working left across the field
red primary read, trails behind into the intermediate window
yellow swings out of the backfield and turns up the right sideline

reads: Work the trail receiver as the primary in the intermediate hole.
If the trail is covered, drop to the shallow drag underneath.
If the defense squeezes the middle, hit the HB wheel up the sideline.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage and zone with a high-low trail concept. Coach's Key: the trail behind the drag is the money read.

name: snapfire_flood

routes.note: yellow stems up then breaks across at intermediate depth
red primary read, pushes vertical up the seam
yellow comes flat to the right sideline at low depth
yellow vertical stretching the right sideline deep

reads: Read the right side flood high to low: deep vertical first.
If the deep route is capped, drop to the intermediate breaking route.
If both are covered, dump the flat route underneath.
usage_notes: Beats: zone coverage by stretching one sideline with three levels. Coach's Key: high-low the flat defender on the trips side.

name: snapfire_pats_hb_screen

routes.note: Red primary ball-carrier path: drifts left behind the line on the screen
Yellow vertical climbing up the left numbers
Yellow breaking out at depth on the right
Yellow stems up then breaks back out to the sideline
Yellow breaking to the sideline at a shorter depth

reads: First look is the HB screen to the left: if the rush crashes upfield and a blocker leads, throw it now.
If the screen lane is covered, climb to the slot wheel up the seam.
Late, work the out routes on the right against soft coverage.
usage_notes: Beats: aggressive man pressure and edge rushers. Coach's Key: let the rush come, then dump it to the back behind the wall.

name: snapfire_wheel_mesh

routes.note: Yellow swings wide left and turns up the left sideline
Red crosser running left to right across the middle: primary read
Yellow shallow crossing right to left, part of the mesh
Yellow stems up then breaks in across the middle at depth
Yellow vertical up the right sideline

reads: Read the mesh first: take the red crosser if a defender trails or the zone opens underneath.
If the linebackers sit on the mesh, drop it over the top to the HB wheel up the left sideline.
Backside go is your one-on-one shot if the corner squats.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage with the rub from the mesh. Coach's Key: the two shallow crossers pick the defenders so the wheel runs free.

name: snapfire_5_6_trap

routes.note: Red ball-carrier path takes the handoff and cuts up through the A/B gap behind the trap block
White blocking bars: line blocks down and a puller traps the unblocked defender

reads: Ride the mesh and read the trapped defender: if he stays put, hit the inside gap.
Follow the puller and get vertical once you clear the line.
Stay tight to the trap block and do not bounce it outside.
usage_notes: Beats: penetrating, upfield defensive tackles. Coach's Key: let the down lineman crash, then trap him and run behind it.

name: snapfire_hb_mid_draw

routes.note: Red ball-carrier path: short delay then straight up the middle through the A gap
White blocking bars: line shows pass before driving the front out of the middle

reads: Sell the pass first, let the rush widen, then take the handoff downhill.
Read the first down lineman: cut to whichever A gap stays clean.
Press the second level and get vertical, do not dance.
usage_notes: Beats: pass-rush-heavy and deep-dropping coverage. Coach's Key: the draw fakes pass to open the middle on the snap.

name: snapfire_cheat_te_hitch_hb_seam

routes.note: Yellow, drives upfield then breaks hard to the left sideline at 12-15 yards
Yellow, short stem then sits at the sticks
Red primary read, releases from the backfield and runs straight up the middle
Cyan motion across the formation that turns up the right hash on a vertical

reads: Pre-snap: take the cyan motion and read how the defense adjusts to it.
IF the middle of the field is open, THEN hit the HB on the seam (the red primary).
IF a defender carries the seam, THEN work the hitch sitting at the sticks.
IF both inside windows are covered, THEN check the deep out to the left.
usage_notes: **Beats:** Single-high middle-open coverage. **Coach's Key:** The HB seam is the primary; the hitch is the timing dump if the middle gets walled off.

name: snapfire_stick

routes.note: Yellow, breaks flat to the left sideline
Red primary, short vertical stem then settles / breaks out at about 5 yards
Yellow, releases out of the backfield and swings to the right flat
Yellow, runs an out to the right sideline above the swing

reads: IF the flat defender sits, THEN throw the stick route settling inside him.
IF the flat defender squeezes the stick, THEN dump it to the HB swinging out.
IF the backside rolls, THEN come back to the out on the right.
Take the easy completion, this is a high-low stick concept on the flat defender.
usage_notes: **Beats:** Zone coverage and flat defenders. **Coach's Key:** Classic stick-flat high-low; read the flat defender and throw off of him.

name: snapfire_escort_power_read_toss

routes.note: The give; the toss widens the sweep track to the edge
Keep downhill through the B gap when the read key widens with the toss
Pull to the play-side linebacker
Bunch receivers block the perimeter for the toss
Backside gap rules with a double team to the backside linebacker

reads: **Pre-Snap:** Find the end man on the line; he is the read key and nobody blocks him
**1️⃣ Read key crashes inside:** toss it, the back gets the edge behind the bunch blocks
**2️⃣ Read key stays outside:** keep it, QB power through the B gap behind the wrap
**🚨 No pass option:** toss or keep, decided by the read key
usage_notes:
**Beats:** Loaded boxes, conservative option defense, defenses that widen with the bunch
**Coach's Key:** Same power read rules with a toss instead of a sweep; the pitch gets the back to the edge faster

name: snapfire_mesh

routes.note: Red, shallow drag rubbing across the middle
Red, opposite shallow drag forming the mesh point
Yellow, breaks out to the left sideline over the top of the mesh
Yellow, swings out of the backfield and turns up the right sideline

reads: Let the two shallow crossers mesh in the middle and create the rub.
IF a crosser comes open running away from his defender, THEN hit him on the move.
IF the crossers are walled, THEN climb to the HB on the wheel up the right sideline.
Use the out to the left as the backside settle versus zone.
usage_notes: **Beats:** Man coverage with the natural rub at the mesh point. **Coach's Key:** Throw the crosser running away from leverage; the wheel is the shot.

name: snapfire_spacing

routes.note: Red primary, quick vertical stem then sits down underneath
Yellow, stems up and curls back at a short depth
Yellow, breaks flat to the left sideline
Yellow, breaks out toward the right sideline
Yellow, releases to the right flat underneath

reads: Read the underneath zones, this is a horizontal spacing concept across the field.
IF the middle hook defender widens, THEN hit the hitch sitting inside (the red).
IF the hitch is covered, THEN work to the curl or out at the next spacing.
Take whatever the underneath defenders leave open at their landmarks.
usage_notes: **Beats:** Zone coverage by flooding the underneath spacing. **Coach's Key:** Quick game rhythm, the inside hitch is the timing throw versus soft zone.

name: snapfire_vertical_te_cross

routes.note: Yellow straight vertical up the left sideline.
Yellow. Stems vertical then breaks back hard to the left across the middle on a deep crossing path.
Yellow stem upfield then sharp break to the left, the crossing read marked by the red arrow toward it.
Red straight vertical up the right sideline, marked as the primary.

reads: First peek the right vertical. If the corner sits and there is grass deep outside, take the shot.
If the deep safety stays over the top, work the crosser coming open across the middle.
If the middle is muddied, check the left Go for a one-on-one outside.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage and single-high. Coach's Key: the crosser is the rhythm answer, the verticals stretch the top off it.

name: snapfire_hb_base

routes.note: Red ball-carrier arrow from the backfield aiming forward and slightly to the right behind the line, an inside base run.
White vertical stalk/block lines from the split receivers, no routes drawn.

reads: Take the snap and follow the red track downhill behind the line.
Read the down block in front and press it tight before bouncing.
If the front clogs, stay patient and fall forward through the crease.
usage_notes: Beats: light boxes. Coach's Key: this is a downhill base run, hit the drawn lane and finish forward.

name: snapfire_fake_jet_pass_power-2

routes.note: Motions right to left in front of the QB to sell the jet sweep
Takes the handoff and follows pullers through the right A/B gap
Down blocks with a puller leading to the playside gap

reads: IF the edge defender chases the jet motion, THEN hand the power inside off his vacated gap.
IF the front slants away from the power, THEN follow the puller and press the hole.
This is a run, do not look to throw it.
usage_notes: Beats: Edge defenders who honor the jet motion, the fake pulls them out of the power gap. Coach's Key: The name says pass, the art is labeled RUN. Sell the jet, then run power. Verified: art labeled RUN, cyan jet motion + red power path + white blocking all drawn.

name: snapfire_midline_read_option-2

routes.note: Ball carrier path straight up the middle through the interior gap
QB pulls and bounces left if the read defender crashes the dive
Interior is blocked, the read defender is left unblocked

reads: IF the read defender crashes the dive, THEN pull and keep to the left edge.
IF the read defender stays home on the QB, THEN give the dive up the middle.
Get downhill, do not dance in the backfield.
usage_notes: Beats: Crashing interior defenders, the midline read makes them wrong either way. Coach's Key: Read the unblocked interior man, give or keep off his leverage. Verified: art labeled RUN, red dive + yellow QB keep-left + white blocking all drawn.

name: snapfire_slant_flat

routes.note: Primary read, breaks in over the middle
Releases to the left flat underneath the slant
Settles over the middle as the hot outlet
Works across the field
Breaks back toward the middle from the right

reads: IF the flat defender widens to the flat, THEN throw the slant behind him.
IF the flat defender sinks under the slant, THEN drop it to the flat.
IF both underneath are covered, THEN check the middle sit route.
usage_notes: Beats: Zone underneath coverage, the slant-flat high-low stresses the flat defender. Coach's Key: Read the flat defender, slant over him or flat under him. Verified: art labeled PASS, red primary slant + flat + middle sit + crossing routes from the right all drawn.

name: snapfire_inside_zone

routes.note: Aims at the playside A gap, cuts back on flow
Linemen step playside in unison and climb to the second level

reads: IF the front flows hard playside, THEN press the hole then cut back to daylight.
IF the interior holds, THEN stay on the zone track and get vertical.
One cut and go, trust the blocks.
usage_notes: Beats: Over-pursuing fronts, the cutback lane opens when defenders chase the zone flow. Coach's Key: Press the A gap, read the first down lineman, one cut downhill. Verified: art labeled RUN, red ball-carrier zone path + white blocking drawn, both listed routes present.

name: snapfire_exit_slot_option

routes.note: Primary side read, breaks across
Works back to the left sideline underneath

routes.route: Exit motion then vertical up the right sideline (cyan)
routes.note: Motions across and turns up the right boundary, the moving target
Comes back toward the ball on the right
Sits or breaks off coverage as the checkdown

reads: IF the slot defender trails the exit motion, THEN hit the vertical up the right sideline.
IF underneath leverage opens, THEN work the left dig or drag.
IF coverage holds deep, THEN settle the back's option route.
usage_notes: Beats: Man coverage on the moving slot, the exit motion creates a free release up the boundary. Coach's Key: The exit motion is the trigger. Art is busy so route detail is low-confidence. Verified: art labeled PASS, all five route colors (two red, cyan exit-motion up right boundary, yellow comeback, blue back) drawn; low confidence honestly reflects the busy art.

name: snapfire_inside_zone_read_opt

routes.note: Red arrow off center showing the QB pull/keep read
Green arrow angling downhill through the interior, the give path
White vertical stem
White vertical stem
White vertical stem on the single side

reads: First step: ride the mesh and eye the backside end.
If the end crashes the RB, pull and keep on the read arrow.
If the end stays home, give it on the inside zone track.
usage_notes: Beats: crashing backside fronts. Coach's Key: the unblocked end decides give or keep, do not predetermine it. VERIFIED: art shows RUN label, red QB keep arrow, green RB give arrow, and three white stalk-block stems (left outside, left slot, right backside). All five routes present, type matches.

name: snapfire_fake_jet_pass_power-3

routes.note: Cyan path sweeping across the formation behind the line, this is the fake
Red arrow showing the ball carrier hitting downhill behind the pulling blocks
White vertical stem on the single side

reads: Sell the jet sweep with the motion man going across.
Hand the power run to the back hitting the red track.
Follow the down blocks and the puller to the play side.
usage_notes: Beats: defenses that chase jet motion. Coach's Key: the motion is a decoy, the power run is the real call (labeled RUN). VERIFIED: art shows RUN label, cyan jet sweep path, short red power arrow, and right-side white stem. All three listed routes present (note: a left-side white stem exists in art but is unlisted, an omission not an invention). Type matches.

name: snapfire_rpo_read_y_flat

routes.note: Yellow break-in route over the middle, the pull-and-throw option if the read pulls.
Yellow route releasing flat toward the play side, the quick RPO option.
Red ball-carrier line through the line of scrimmage, the give read.

reads: Read the run key first: if the box is light, hand it off on the red HB track.
If the key crashes the run, pull and hit the Y on the flat route.
If the flat is covered, the backside slant is the next window over the middle.
usage_notes: Beats: loaded boxes that vacate the flat. Coach's Key: the flat is the answer, run is the conflict. Verified: RPO label matches, all three routes drawn (yellow break-in, yellow flat, red HB track).

name: snapfire_rpo_bubble_y_pop

routes.note: Yellow bubble release flaring back and out toward the bottom, the quick screen option.
Yellow route stemming vertical then breaking back inside, the pop window over the middle.
Red break-in line over the middle, the backside read.

reads: Pre-snap read the numbers: if outnumbered outside, throw the bubble now.
If the defender sits on the bubble, pop it up to the inside breaking route.
If the box overcommits play side, the backside slant on the red line is open.
usage_notes: Beats: defenses that fail to fit the bubble or that crash the box. Coach's Key: bubble first, pop the Y when they jump the flat. Verified: RPO label matches, yellow bubble + yellow pop + red backside break-in all drawn.

name: snapfire_cheat_te_angle

routes.note: Yellow line straight up the left sideline
Yellow, releases up then breaks out to the sideline at the second level
Yellow, shorter out underneath the first out
Cyan motion across, then yellow angle route releasing toward the deep middle
Yellow line up the right sideline
Blue line out toward the right flat from the backfield

reads: IF the angle route from the motion man clears the linebacker level, THEN hit him crossing the middle.
IF the defense carries the angle, THEN climb to one of the vertical outs on the left.
IF everything downfield is covered, THEN dump the RB on the flat to the right.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage and zone droppers that lose the motion man. Coach's Key: send the cyan motion first and read the angle off the underneath defender.

name: snapfire_mtn_hb_angle

routes.note: Yellow breaking back to the left sideline
Yellow, drives up then breaks out to the left
Yellow up the right hash
Cyan motion into a yellow short curl-back over the middle right
Cyan release then angle from the backfield toward the right

reads: IF the RB angle is open underneath, THEN take the easy throw.
IF the underneath defender sits on the angle, THEN work the Y settle/curl over the middle.
IF coverage rolls down, THEN climb to the vertical on the right.
usage_notes: Beats: man and zone that chase the back. Coach's Key: motion the back-side man, then read the HB angle against the flat defender first.

name: snapfire_mtn_hb_swing

routes.note: Yellow short release that breaks back to the inside
Yellow shallow crossing flat across to the right sideline
Cyan swing out of the backfield to the left flat

reads: IF the RB swing has space to the left flat, THEN take it and let him run.
IF the flat defender jumps the swing, THEN come back to the Y whip/return over the middle.
IF both are covered, THEN climb to the shallow crosser to the right.
usage_notes: Beats: zone that vacates the flat. Coach's Key: get the back on the perimeter fast and let blockers set up in front.

name: snapfire_cheat_te_screen

routes.note: Yellow up the right sideline to clear space
Two short white/gray vertical-hitch stems to the left, clearing the area
Cyan motion back across, then settles as the screen target behind the line over the middle

reads: IF the box flows away from the screen, THEN deliver to the TE behind the wall.
IF rushers stay home over the screen, THEN pull the ball down or throw it away.
IF the screen is covered, THEN check the clear-out hitches.
usage_notes: Beats: heavy pressure and aggressive edge rushers. Coach's Key: sell the drop, then flip the TE screen behind the blockers.

name: snapfire_wheel_switch

routes.note: Yellow breaking flat to the left sideline
Yellow stem up then bending over the top to the left deep
Yellow short curl then wheeling up the right sideline (switch release)
Red line straight up the right hash, the primary read
Yellow release wide then wheeling up the right sideline behind the outside vertical

reads: IF the red vertical/seam splits the safeties, THEN take the primary up the right.
IF the safety caps the seam, THEN throw the wheel underneath off the switch release.
IF the right side is bracketed, THEN work back to the X out on the left.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage on the switch and single-high looks. Coach's Key: read the red vertical first, then drop to the wheel on the right.

name: snapfire_y_stick

routes.note: Yellow angling up and out to the left sideline
Yellow drive up then breaking in across the middle
Red short stick, the primary read, sits and turns just past the sticks
Yellow drive up then break out to the right sideline

reads: IF the flat defender widens, THEN hit the Y stick sitting inside.
IF the defender squeezes the stick, THEN go to the out behind it on the right.
IF coverage is tight underneath, THEN climb to the dig over the middle.
usage_notes: Beats: zone coverage and soft flats. Coach's Key: red stick is the answer, read the flat defender high to low.

name: snapfire_reload_pa_cross

routes.note: Yellow up then break out to the left sideline
Red crossing route from left to right, climbing to the second level, the primary read
Yellow up the right sideline
Blue shallow crosser working left underneath
Cyan release after the fake, leaking across to the right flat

reads: IF the play-action holds the linebackers, THEN hit the red deep cross in the window.
IF the cross is covered, THEN drop to the shallow drag underneath.
IF the middle is muddy, THEN climb to the right vertical or check the back.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage and aggressive run defenders. Coach's Key: sell the play fake, then read the deep cross over the shallow.

name: snapfire_empty_mtn_jailbreak_screen

routes.note: Yellow straight up the left sideline
Short yellow hitch settling near the line
Cyan screen, receiver sets up wide right with the line releasing to block
Cyan shallow release across to set the jailbreak wall to the right

reads: IF the corner plays off the screen receiver, THEN throw the jailbreak right and follow the wall.
IF the corner sits on the screen, THEN pull it down or hit the clear-out hitch.
IF pressure comes fast, THEN get the ball out to the screen now.
usage_notes: Beats: blitz-heavy looks and soft corners. Coach's Key: empty set spreads the box, then flip the screen to the wall side.

name: snapfire_inside_zone-2

routes.note: White stems, line steps in unison and zone blocks
Red arrow, ball-carrier presses the interior gap and reads the front

reads: IF the front-side gap opens, THEN press it and get downhill.
IF the front-side is clogged, THEN cut back to the backside lane.
IF the defense over-pursues, THEN bounce and find the soft edge.
usage_notes: Beats: even fronts and undisciplined gap fits. Coach's Key: one cut and go, read the first down lineman.

name: snapfire_smash_return

routes.note: Yellow stem releases and breaks back toward the sideline at the second level.
Yellow short stem with a hook back, sitting in front of the line.
Yellow vertical climbing up the field on the left side.
Red primary read: works out and snaps back inside toward the ball, the smash return key.
Red angle breaking up and out toward the right sideline.
Yellow path out of the backfield bending up the right sideline.

reads: IF the right-side red return defender sits underneath, THEN climb to the corner over the top.
IF the deep corner squeezes the boundary, THEN drop the ball on the returning route in the void.
IF the underneath is muddy on the right, THEN reset to the curl or out on the left.
IF nothing opens fast, THEN check the back leaking up the sideline.
usage_notes: Beats: zone that turns its back to the comeback. Coach's Key: the red return route is the trigger, high-low it with the corner.

name: snapfire_mtn_slot_choice

routes.note: Cyan line running flat across the field from left to right.
Yellow stem up then breaking out toward the left sideline.
Cyan release with a short blue tick at the break, the choice receiver reading leverage.
Yellow vertical climbing hard up the right side.
Yellow line breaking flat across at intermediate depth to the right.

reads: IF the choice defender plays soft, THEN sit the option underneath.
IF the defender presses or sits down, THEN break it vertical past him.
IF the right vertical wins leverage, THEN take the shot up the seam.
IF coverage rallies inside, THEN dump the shallow cross moving across the field.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage on the choice slot. Coach's Key: the cyan choice route is your option read, let the defender pick his poison.

name: snapfire_flood-2

routes.note: Yellow stem then break out toward the left sideline at mid depth.
Yellow climbing straight up the left sideline (clear-out).
Yellow short release out to the flat underneath.
Red primary read: vertical stem breaking out and up toward the right sideline.
Yellow line running flat across to the right at intermediate depth.

reads: IF the flat defender carries the corner, THEN the flat opens underneath.
IF the corner sinks deep, THEN throw the intermediate out in front of him.
IF the flood floods one side, THEN work high to low: corner, out, flat.
IF the boundary is covered, THEN reset to the backside out.
usage_notes: Beats: zone coverage to the flood side. Coach's Key: three levels on the right, read the flat defender's depth.

name: snapfire_mtn_x_dig_slot_corner

routes.note: Yellow stem up then breaking flat across the middle to the right (the X dig).
Cyan line running flat across the field underneath.
Yellow stem breaking out and up toward the right sideline (the slot corner).
Red primary read: vertical climbing up and bending inside over the top.

reads: IF the safety jumps the dig, THEN hit the slot corner outside of him.
IF the corner widens on the slot, THEN the red vertical post opens up the middle.
IF the middle is filled, THEN settle the dig in the window.
IF coverage drops deep, THEN check the shallow drag underneath.
usage_notes: Beats: single-high middle coverage. Coach's Key: the dig and slot corner stress the same defender, read his leverage.

name: snapfire_empty_mtn_flood_h_trail

routes.note: Yellow climbing up the left sideline (clear-out).
Yellow stem then breaking flat in toward the middle from the left.
Second yellow breaking flat across to the left at a similar depth (flood the middle).
Cyan release working up and trailing back in behind the dig (the H-trail).
Blue line working flat across from the right side.

reads: IF the underneath defenders sink with the digs, THEN the H-trail comes open behind them.
IF the middle is crowded, THEN work the in-breakers across the second level.
IF the boundary clears out, THEN take the vertical up the sideline.
IF pressure comes from empty, THEN hit the first crosser moving across your face.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage from an empty look. Coach's Key: empty set, the trailing cyan route works behind traffic, let the digs clear the box.

name: snapfire_qb_power_rpo

routes.note: Green run arrow up the middle through the designed power lane. Red give/pass key on the backside decides keep vs throw.
White blocking lines collapse the front to open the inside run lane for the QB.
White vertical block, seals the edge on the run side.
White line curls down to wall off the inside defender.
Red arrow breaking to the inside, this is the pass read off the RPO. Primary key.

reads: IF the backside box defender crashes the QB run, THEN pull and hit the red drag working inside.
IF the box stays light, THEN keep it and run QB Power behind the down blocks.
IF the drag is covered and the box is loaded, THEN tuck and take what the blocks give you up the middle.
usage_notes: Beats: light boxes and crashing edge defenders. Coach's Key: read the backside box player, the red drag is the giveaway throw when he commits to the run.

name: snapfire_whip_double_spot

routes.note: Yellow stem that breaks flat to the left sideline
Yellow vertical stem that hooks back up
Yellow short hook over the ball
Yellow stem breaking out to the right
Red carrier line running across behind the line to the right sideline

reads: If the flat defender sinks, hit the HB on the red swing to the right.
If the corner and out clear the right side, work the bunch spot sit underneath.
If single coverage shows backside, take the whip out to the left sideline.
Late, climb to the curl as the dump down.
usage_notes: Beats: zone coverage. Coach's Key: high-low the flat defender with the swing and the spot sit, read it out to the whip side.

name: snapfire_drive_hb_under

routes.note: Yellow vertical up the left sideline
Yellow route working across the middle
Yellow vertical up the field
Yellow vertical up the field
Red carrier line running shallow under the routes from right to left

reads: If the verticals clear out the middle, hit the HB on the red under route.
If a linebacker chases the under, work the drive dig in behind him.
If the deep coverage rolls, take the backside go up the left sideline.
Check the bunch verticals against soft coverage.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage. Coach's Key: clear with the verticals, drive the HB underneath into the vacated middle.

name: snapfire_verticals

routes.note: Red line, straight vertical up the left sideline, primary read
Yellow, straight vertical
Yellow, up then breaks back inside left at the second level
Yellow, vertical with a slight bend
Yellow, swings right out of the backfield then up the right sideline

reads: If the safety sits on the left vertical, hit the inside dig underneath.
If single-high rotates, take the bender up the seam.
If the flat defender chases the dig, the wheel is one-on-one.
usage_notes: Beats: single-high and man. Coach's Key: four verticals plus a wheel stresses the deep middle, work the dig as the outlet.

name: snapfire_over_pivot

routes.note: Red line, vertical up the left sideline, primary
Yellow, short break
Yellow, vertical then breaks deep right
Yellow, drives up then out toward the right sideline
Blue motion line, out to the right flat then pivots back inside

reads: If the corner squats, take the go up the left.
If the underneath defender widens with the out, the pivot returns inside.
If the safety jumps the post, check the intermediate out.
usage_notes: Beats: man. Coach's Key: the pivot return punishes defenders chasing the outbreaking routes.

name: snapfire_post_rail

routes.note: Yellow, short crosser to the right
Yellow, short in-breaker behind the first drag
Yellow, vertical up the left numbers, deep
Red line, vertical then deep middle, primary
Yellow, vertical up the right then corner

reads: If the deep middle is open, throw the TE post.
If the safety caps the post, take the rail.
If both deep are covered, drop to the drags.
usage_notes: Beats: Cover 2 and Cover 3. Coach's Key: post-rail high-low on the safety, drags underneath.

name: snapfire_mtn_dagger_sit

routes.note: Red primary read, breaks back across the middle from depth
Yellow, clears straight up to occupy the deep defender
Yellow, sits then works out toward the right sideline
Cyan motion across the formation before the snap, settles as the underneath sit route
Yellow, releases underneath toward the right

reads: IF the deep defender carries the vertical seam, THEN throw the dig coming back over the middle.
IF the middle is jammed, THEN drop it down to the sit route underneath.
IF the flat defender squats, THEN look to the out route to the right sideline.
usage_notes: Beats: zone coverage. Coach's Key: the vertical clears the window so the dig and the underneath sit work the high-low.

name: snapfire_mtn_drive_zig

routes.note: Cyan motion underneath pre-snap, then breaks out to the left
Yellow, stems vertical then breaks back inside at the top
Yellow, pushes up then comes back across to the middle
Yellow, stems up then works toward the right sideline at depth
Yellow, shallow release working to the right

reads: IF the deep defenders sink, THEN hit the dig coming back over the middle.
IF the underneath flat is open, THEN take the drag working across.
IF coverage rolls one way, THEN work the backside out route.
usage_notes: Beats: split-field zone. Coach's Key: the crossing dig and the underneath drag flood the middle while the outs stretch the sidelines. Art lines overlap in the middle so routes are read with lower certainty.

name: snapfire_dagger

routes.note: Red primary read, climbs then breaks hard back across the middle
Yellow, runs straight up the seam to clear the safety
Yellow, takes the top off on the right
Yellow, breaks inside under the verticals
Yellow, releases underneath toward the left

reads: IF the seam runner clears the safety, THEN drop the dig in behind the linebackers.
IF the middle linebacker widens, THEN the dig opens up right away.
IF defenders pass the dig off deep, THEN check the underneath route.
usage_notes: Beats: single-high man and zone. Coach's Key: classic dagger, the vertical seam runs the safety off and the dig sits in the vacated window.

name: snapfire_wr_cross

routes.note: yellow, breaks out to the left flat just past the line
red primary read, releases from the left and crosses to the deep middle, ball-carrier red arrow finishing up-field right of center
yellow, stems vertical then breaks out toward the right sideline
blue, releases from the backfield out to the right toward the flat

reads: If the deep cross (red) clears the linebackers and finds the open window, take it.
If the middle is muddy, check the right out as it breaks toward the sideline.
If both are covered, dump to the RB releasing into the right flat.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage and soft zones over the middle. Coach's Key: the red crosser is the primary, work the underneath out and RB checkdown if it is taken away.

name: snapfire_jet_power_read

routes.note: Full-speed motion; the give, and the handoff is automatic when the read key crashes
Keep through the B gap when the read key stays outside
Leaks out to block the safety for the sweep
Pull to the play-side linebacker
Down blocks with a double team up to the backside linebacker

reads: **Pre-Snap:** Find the end man on the line of scrimmage; he is the read key and nobody blocks him
**1️⃣ Read key crashes inside:** the jet sweep handoff happens automatically, perimeter blockers are already out front
**2️⃣ Read key stays outside:** keep it, QB power through the B gap behind the wrap
**🚨 No pass option:** jet sweep or QB power, the read key decides
usage_notes:
**Beats:** Loaded boxes, edge defenders who chase jet motion, goal line fronts
**Coach's Key:** Opponents who think the jet is a fake will crash inside and hand the sweep the edge; this play stays solid near the goal line

name: snapfire_hb_quick_base

routes.note: Red ball-carrier path angles from the backfield up into the line behind the center

reads: IF the front gives ground at the mesh, THEN press downhill behind the line on the red path.
IF the interior clogs, THEN bounce off the block to the nearest soft gap.
usage_notes: **Beats:** Light interior fronts. **Coach's Key:** Quick-hitting base run, follow the red path north and take what the line gives.

name: snapfire_mtn_rpo_alert_y_flat

routes.note: Cyan path runs flat across the formation from the left
Short yellow route breaking down and out toward the flat
Red deep route up the right sideline as the alert read

reads: IF the box is light, THEN hand off on the inside run action.
IF the flat defender sits, THEN throw the Y flat for easy yards.
IF the deep right is single covered, THEN alert the red vertical.
usage_notes: **Beats:** Defenses that crash the run and leave the flat or sideline open. **Coach's Key:** Read run first, then peek the Y flat, alert the deep shot only when it's there.

name: snapfire_mtn_rpo_read_slide

routes.note: Cyan path runs flat across the field from the left toward the right sideline
Second cyan path comes underneath toward the same crossing lane
Short yellow ball-carrier path into the line

reads: IF the read defender stays home on the run, THEN slide the ball to the crossing receiver.
IF the box opens up, THEN give the inside run on the yellow path.
IF the slide receiver is covered, THEN check the second crosser underneath.
usage_notes: **Beats:** Run-committed fronts that vacate the underneath crossing window. **Coach's Key:** Read the conflict defender, give on a soft box, slide it when he sits.

name: snapfire_qb_blast

routes.note: QB ball-carrier path drives downhill through the interior with a lead/pull blocker ahead
Back's path shown as a white blocking lane in front of the QB

reads: IF the interior is light, THEN run the QB straight downhill behind the lead.
IF the front squeezes, THEN follow the lead block to the open gap.
usage_notes: **Beats:** Boxes that don't account for the QB as a runner. **Coach's Key:** QB run behind the lead, hit it north and physical.

name: snapfire_levels_y_sail

routes.note: Yellow. Pushes off the line then breaks square to the middle.
Red primary read. Stems vertical a few yards then breaks hard across the middle to the right.
Yellow. Short break to the middle underneath.
Yellow. Gets vertical then bends out and up toward the right sideline deep.
Yellow. Pushes upfield then hitches back toward the sideline.

reads: First eyes go to the red crossing route working across the middle.
If the crosser is covered, climb to the sail breaking out and up on the right.
If both are taken, check the underneath in / drag for a quick completion.
usage_notes: Beats: zone coverage with a high-low stretch on the same side. Coach's Key: the red crosser is the primary, the sail is the deep answer. Art verified: PASS label, red crosser breaks right across the middle, both the deep sail and the lower comeback are drawn on the right.

name: snapfire_smash_y_shake

routes.note: Yellow. Short stem then turns back with a small flag tail.
Red primary read. Releases vertical straight up the field.
Yellow. Runs straight up the field.
Yellow. Gets vertical then breaks back hard to the left across the middle.
Yellow. Pushes upfield then settles and hooks back.

reads: Work the red vertical first against single coverage.
If the deep ball is not there, come down to the dig breaking back to the middle.
Take the underneath hitch or curl as the checkdown.
usage_notes: Beats: man and single-high looks with a vertical clearout plus a dig underneath. Coach's Key: the red vertical sets up the in-breaker behind it. Art verified: PASS label, red runs vertical, the mid-field yellow dig arrow points back left across the middle, right-side curl settles.

name: snapfire_pa_scissors_crossers

routes.note: Yellow. Climbs and bends across to the deep middle.
Red primary read. Works back toward the left sideline at a shorter depth.
Yellow. Crosses up and out toward the deep right.
Yellow. Stems and crosses the field to the left.
Yellow. Shallow crosser working back to the left underneath.

reads: After the play-action fake, read the crossing scissors concept high to low.
If the deep crossers are covered, drop to the red comeback on the left.
Take the shallow drag underneath if everything deep is bracketed.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage with crossing rubs and a play-action hold. Coach's Key: the routes overlap so confirm leverage before the throw (art is busy, read carefully). Art verified: PASS label, busy overlapping crossers consistent with the concept, red route works back toward the left short. Confidence honestly kept low given the crossing clutter.

name: snapfire_y_corner

routes.note: Yellow stem upfield then hooks back inside to settle.
Yellow route working back across the field to the left.
Yellow route also crossing left, layered under the higher drag.
Red primary read: vertical stem then breaks deep toward the right sideline.

reads: First, eye the Corner route from the widest trips receiver. If the deep defender stays inside or sits, throw the corner up the sideline.
If the corner is bracketed, check the two crossing drags coming back to the left and hit the one that clears traffic.
If nothing inside is open, work back to the backside Curl as the outlet.
usage_notes: Beats: single-high and soft outside leverage. Coach's Key: corner is the money route off the trips bunch, the drags pull underneath defenders away from it.

name: snapfire_corner_go_s_flat

routes.note: Yellow. Breaks flat toward the left sideline.
Yellow. Runs across the field underneath toward the left.
Yellow. Pushes straight upfield.
Yellow. Stems up then breaks back out toward the right sideline.
Red ball marker. Releases to the right flat after checking protection.

reads: IF the deep go up the right side is one-on-one, THEN take the shot vertical.
IF the safety carries the go, THEN drop it down to the shallow crosser working open underneath.
IF the flat defender squeezes inside, THEN hit the back releasing to the flat.
usage_notes: Beats: single-high man. Coach's Key: the vertical clears the deep zone so the crosser and flat win underneath. Verified: art labeled PASS; left flat, left shallow cross, right vertical, right flat-out, and red RB flat all drawn. No invented routes.

name: snapfire_rpo_read_cntr

routes.note: Red ball marker. Run track tracks back across the formation to the right.
Yellow. Short out cut, the bubble/flat read.
Yellow. Stems up and breaks out, the pull-and-throw read.

reads: IF the read defender crashes the counter run, THEN pull and throw the quick out.
IF the read defender sits / widens to the throw, THEN give the counter run.
IF the flat is jumped, THEN tuck the give inside on the counter track.
usage_notes: Beats: a free defender on the edge. Coach's Key: read the conflict defender, give versus widen, pull versus crash. Verified: art labeled RPO; red counter run track plus two yellow out/break routes drawn. No invented routes.

name: snapfire_bench_dig_curl

routes.note: Red. Primary, runs straight up the left sideline.
Yellow. Breaks flat to the left sideline.
Yellow. Pushes upfield then digs back across to the left.
Yellow. Drives upfield then curls back to settle.
Blue. Releases out of the backfield and bends up the right sideline.

reads: IF the deep corner is off, THEN take the vertical down the left sideline.
IF the vertical is covered, THEN work the dig crossing the middle.
IF the underneath defenders sink, THEN hit the curl settling in the soft spot.
IF nothing opens up, THEN check it down to the back out of the backfield.
usage_notes: Beats: zone coverage. Coach's Key: dig and curl flood the intermediate middle while the vertical holds the top. Verified: art labeled PASS; red vertical, yellow left out, yellow dig, yellow curl, and blue RB wheel/swing all drawn. No invented routes.

name: snapfire_pa_y_cross

routes.note: Yellow vertical stem straight up the left sideline
Yellow route releasing inside and bending across the field on a deep diagonal
Red line working back across to the left at intermediate depth, marked as the key read
Yellow route breaking out toward the right sideline underneath
Backfield dot with a short yellow release into the flat after the fake

reads: First peek the red crosser coming back to the left, if he clears the underneath defender throw it on time
If the crosser is covered, climb to the deep cross splitting the middle
If the middle is muddy, dump the flat route to the right for the easy gain
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage and single-high looks. Coach's Key: sell the run fake first, the red crosser is your timing read.

name: snapfire_speed_option

routes.note: Red ball-carrier path attacking the edge to the left off the snap
Yellow path arcing wide left to stay in pitch relationship with the QB

reads: Snap and attack the edge, eye the unblocked force defender
If he sits or plays the pitch, keep it and turn upfield
If he crashes the QB, pitch it to the back running the yellow arc
usage_notes: Beats: aggressive edge defenders who crash inside. Coach's Key: option off the force player, make him wrong every time.

name: snapfire_curl_switch

routes.note: Red primary read. Pushes upfield then hooks back to the sideline.
Yellow. Releases straight up the field.
Yellow. Drives upfield then breaks back across the middle to the left.
Yellow. Settles and hooks back toward the quarterback.
Yellow. Releases inside and runs the deep crossing route all the way to the left.

reads: Pre-snap: read the leverage on the right trey stack.
IF the left curl (red) sits open against soft coverage, THEN take it for the high-percentage throw.
IF the curl is covered, THEN work the right-side dig coming open across the middle.
IF the underneath is muddied, THEN climb to the deep crosser running away from leverage.
usage_notes: Beats: zone coverage. Coach's Key: the left curl is the layup, the right-side dig and crosser flood the middle behind it.

name: snapfire_jet_qb_zone

routes.note: Takes the zone path behind the line off the jet action
Motions across the formation, a decoy that pulls the edge defender
Covered drive, uncovered combo and climb to the linebacker
Wall off the perimeter defenders

reads: **Pre-Snap:** Empty QB-run look, the jet motion stretches the edge and counts a hat for the box
**1️⃣ Jet pulls the edge:** the QB keeps on the zone path inside
**Back's read:** bang the A gap, bend the B gap, bounce outside if the front overflows
usage_notes:
**Beats:** Light boxes and edge defenders who chase the jet motion
**Coach's Key:** Needs a mobile QB, this is a quarterback-carrier run

name: snapfire_jet_qb_zone_wk

routes.note: Runs the zone path to the weak side off the jet action
Motions across to hold the play-side edge
Covered drive, uncovered combo and climb
Wall the perimeter

reads: **Pre-Snap:** The weak-side mirror of Jet QB Zone, call it to run away from the defense's strength
**1️⃣ Jet holds the edge:** keep the QB on the weak-side zone path
**Back's read:** bang, bend, bounce off the line's movement
usage_notes:
**Beats:** Defenses that set strength to the jet motion, you run away from it
**Coach's Key:** Mobile QB required

name: snapfire_hb_counter_u

routes.note: Two white vertical blocking stems walling off defenders downfield
White down-blocking angle to the right with the line stepping that way
Red primary, takes the handoff and cuts back to the left behind the pull

reads: Open the give to the right to sell flow, then counter back left
Follow the pulling blocker through the hole
If the front overflows, press it back inside off the down blocks
usage_notes:
**Beats:** Fast-flowing fronts that chase initial action
**Coach's Key:** This is a run, the misdirection sets up the cutback

name: snapfire_mtn_hb_base

routes.note: White vertical blocking stem up the left side
Cyan short motion then a flat sealing path across
Red primary, downhill run angling up and to the right behind the lead

reads: Take the handoff and follow the lead blocker downhill
Read the playside block and press the open gap
Cut off the seal if the front squeezes the hole
usage_notes:
**Beats:** Light boxes that give up the playside gap
**Coach's Key:** This is a base power run, hit it downhill and read your lead

name: snapfire_mtn_hb_wheel

routes.note: Cyan motion across the formation then turning up the sideline on a wheel
Blue route breaking back up and out to the sideline
Yellow stem up the left side
Red route working down the left edge as a deep target

reads: Watch the wheel develop off the motion up the sideline
If the flat defender carries the wheel, the underneath blue route opens
Take the backside vertical or comeback if the wheel side is capped
usage_notes:
**Beats:** Defenders who squat on the flat and get beat up the sideline
**Coach's Key:** Let the wheel clear, throw it over the trailing defender

name: snapfire_speed_option-2

routes.note: Two white vertical blocking stems walling off defenders
Red primary, attacks the left edge reading the pitch key
Yellow pitch path running outside and to the left in pitch relationship

reads: Attack the edge defender on the option
If he plays the QB, pitch it to the yellow back
If he widens to the pitch, keep it and turn upfield
usage_notes:
**Beats:** Slow or undisciplined edge defenders forced to choose
**Coach's Key:** This is a run, get downhill and make the key wrong

name: snapfire_spacing_x_wheel

routes.note: Red primary read, short stem then turns up the field along the left numbers
Yellow, climbs vertically up the left side
Yellow, breaks out toward the left sideline underneath
Yellow, short hook settling over the ball
Yellow, runs the full width of the field across the intermediate level

reads: Work the red wheel first: if the defender squats on the underneath spacing, the wheel runs by him up the sideline.
If the wheel is carried, drop your eyes to the spacing triangle (flat, curl) and take the open horizontal window.
If everything underneath is covered, the backside over route is your late crossing answer.
usage_notes: Beats: zone and squat coverage. Coach's Key: the spacing routes hold underneath defenders flat-footed so the wheel can climb behind them.

name: snapfire_te_slot_cross

routes.note: Yellow stem up then hooks back to the left sideline.
Yellow shallow drag working left underneath.
Yellow short route breaking back to the left across the formation.
Red primary read, stems up then crosses the field to the left at depth.
Yellow vertical stem breaking back toward the middle.

reads: Work the red TE crosser across the middle as the primary.
If the crosser is bracketed, drop to the shallow drag underneath.
The comeback and back-side breaker are the outlets if the middle is flooded.
usage_notes: Beats: man coverage with a rub. Coach's Key: the TE crosser off the shallow drag stresses defenders crossing through traffic.

name: snapfire_dbl_ins

routes.note: Yellow stem upfield then hooks back to the left.
Yellow drives up then breaks in to the left across the middle.
Red primary read, shallow route working left underneath across the formation.
Yellow stem up then breaks back inside upfield.
Yellow drives up then breaks in to the left across the middle.

reads: Start on the red shallow drag working left underneath.
Climb to the dig routes breaking in over the middle if the drag is covered.
